spec_match.rationale: The self.origin check is correct, but the BroadcastChannel check assumes the constructor will throw on opaque origins (which it does not), leading to a false-negative where compliant Chrome 150+ environments are flagged as failing same-origin isolation.

interactive.rationale: Provides a direct 'Run' button that spins up a live data: URL worker, parses returned results, and populates a results grid.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches the index blurb's target of probing self.origin and related storage APIs live.

edge_cases.rationale: Wrongly evaluates successful BroadcastChannel instantiation as a security gap, rather than checking whether messages actually route between page and worker.

references.rationale: The page lacks a references list or see also block and does not cite the required external ChromeStatus link.

design_system.rationale: Applies clean grid columns, neat comparison box code blocks, and correct variable styling.

openQuestions.title: Fix the false-negative BroadcastChannel logic check
openQuestions.detail: The BroadcastChannel constructor does not throw on opaque origins in spec-compliant browsers; instead, it isolates the channel's namespace. Update the worker test code to match communication-tester: open a BroadcastChannel in both the page and the worker, attempt to send a message from the worker, and check if the page fails to receive it after a timeout.

openQuestions.title: Cite the ChromeStatus tracking entry
openQuestions.detail: Add the required external reference link (https://chromestatus.com/feature/6290352295247872) at the bottom of the page to fulfill the showcase quality rules.
